Responsible AI
NExT 2026 Workshop, Singapore
Wed Apr 29 - Fri May 1, 2026

We are pleased to announce the NExT Singapore Workshop 2026, which will take place from April 29 to May 1, 2026. The theme of this year’s workshop is “Responsible AI”, featuring a series of focused activities:

    • 25-28 Apr: Pre-workshop event - RAi Sandpit Workshop. (Associated event organized by Prof. Chua Tat-Seng and Prof. Wendy Hall). Please note that the RAi Workshop is strictly by invitation only, with the exception of the last day.
    • 29-30 Apr: NExT Workshop focusing on "Responsible AI".
    • 1 May: Discussion on AI trends and future planning for NExT Workshops.

This workshop is designed to bring together NExT members and collaborators to explore the advancements, applications, and future pathways of Responsible AI. Participation is by invitation only, with registration limited to invitees.

More details will be shared soon—stay tuned! We look forward to seeing you in Singapore.

Registration

Registration (Google Form)

Hotel

The designated official workshop hotel is Hilton Singapore Orchard (333 Orchard Rd, Singapore 238867) . You can book a room at the hotel through the link: https://www.hilton.com/en/hotels/sinorhi-hilton-singapore-orchard/.

Program

TBD. More details will be shared soon—stay tuned!

Social Event

30 Apr 2026: Outdoor Walk & Dinner

We will gather at Changi Sailing Club (32 Netheravon Rd, Singapore 508508) for a scenic coastal walk along the Changi Point Coastal Walk — enjoy fresh sea breezes and beautiful views of yachts moored in the marina. After the walk, we will head to dinner at Georges @ Changi (Seafix Restaurant), a popular restaurant just nearby.

Changi Point Coastal Walk  ·  Trail Guide ↗

Changi Sailing Club

Georges @ Changi (Seafix Restaurant)

1 May 2026: Half-day Tour

We will spend the morning at Rainforest Wild ASIA (20 Mandai Lake Road, Singapore 729825), Asia's first wildlife adventure park opened in March 2025. Spanning 13 hectares of lush greenery, the park features eight immersive zones home to 36 species including the Malayan tiger, sun bear, François' langur and the Philippine spotted deer. Explore the elevated canopy walkway for sweeping rainforest views, or take the Orange Trek across logs and boulders for a closer encounter with nature.

Address
20 Mandai Lake Road, Singapore 729825

Official Site

Wildlife photos: guineapig33 (CC BY-NC-ND 4.0) & Wikimedia Commons (CC BY-SA 4.0)

Location

The workshop will be held at Innovation 4.0, NUS (3 Research Link, Singapore 117602).
